notes: SHAFT SECTION, not a boring. Header: 'SECTION OF Durham Main Colliery, Framwellgate' (the BGS index spells it 'FRANWELLGATE'; the scan clearly reads 'Framwellgate') with grid 'NZ 2741 4349' written below (consistent with the header's 427416,543495). Surface Level '200 A.O.D.' (ft). 'Communicated ... by B.G.S. 2661' (cursive, reading uncertain; likely a records reference). Date of boring or sinking and Borer both dashed out ('-'); year unknown. One-inch Map 26; Six-inch Map Durham 20 SW. The blue printed form itself carries a '2/49' (Feb 1949) printer's imprint — the form's print date, NOT the sinking date. Top-right of p1 an old registration number is crossed out and renumbered '17', with pencil folio '36'; p2 carries pencil folio '37' and a pencil carry-forward depth 52-4-8 above the depth columns. STRUCTURE: line 1 lumps surface-to-Hutton-thill (17-5-11) with no itemised strata; intervals 2-7 are shaft furniture (three 'Walling' masonry-lining rows, classed 'unknown', and a 'Sump', classed 'void'), the two 'Post' rows among them being the natural sandstone the shaft passed through. The detailed strata from interval 8 down to 65 fathoms read like an underground borehole put down from the shaft bottom (sump). CLERK ARITHMETIC IS CLEAN: all 50 rows check exactly (previous depth + thickness = stated depth, fathom = 6 ft), no sum slips. Two independent transcription passes agree on every interval's verbatim text, class, thickness and depth. SEAM CORRELATION DISCREPANCY: the driller's own text brackets intervals 42-46 (~62 fm) as the 'Busty Seam', but a later BGS geologist pencilled 'U BT' beside the coal at interval 30 (~54 fm) and 'L BT' beside the coal at interval 37 (~57 fm) — most plausibly Upper/Lower Busty correlations placing the Busty ~7 fathoms higher than the driller. The abbreviation expansion is genuinely uncertain; a prior pass read these (plus two more marks at intervals 20 and 27) as Tilley-group ('U/L TTi', 'U/L BTi'), but contrast-enhanced crops show (a) the two legible marks read 'BT' with no clear terminal 'i', (b) no legible pencil at intervals 20 or 27 (dust only), and (c) a Tilley correlation would be stratigraphically impossible here (Tilley sits well below the Busty). Base of log 65-0-2 = 390.17 ft = 118.92 m; the BGS index depth 118.87 m corresponds to 65 fm 0 ft (390 ft) exactly, i.e. it drops the trailing 2 inches.
